items.transcript: So here's my list of expensive things I think are worth it. A good accountant. Buying fabric and having things made. Anything from Dohan. Paner Aqua Water. YSL loafers. A cleaner. Drawer dividers. A good washing machine and separate dryer. Mother jeans. A portable air conditioner. Augustinus Bader The Rich Cream. Whisternus Bader The Face Oil. Charlie Bingham's ready meals. Bean to cup coffee machine. A stand mixer. A Kindle Scribe Coloursoft. A Dualit toaster. Good garden furniture. Magazine subscriptions. Piglet in a bed bedding. Rise and fall bedding. Walker's crisps. The Crusade butter dish. Arga pan set. M&S food shop. Hourglass bronzer. And Kerastase mask intense conditioner.

items.transcript: I love this trend. So here's my list of expensive things I think are worth it. Possibly part one. A good accountant, buying fabric and having things made. Just get more choice. Anything from Doen, Pana Aqua Water, YSL loafers, a cleaner, drawer dividers, a good washing machine and separate dryer, mother jeans, a portable air conditioner, or in the future just air conditioning, Augustinus Bader the Rich Cream, Augustinus Bader the Face Oil, Charlie Bingham's Ready Meals, or Bingham's, Bingham's Bingham's, a bean-to-cup coffee machine, a stand mixer, a Kindle Scribe Coloursoft, a dual-lit toaster, good garden furniture, magazine subscriptions, piglet-in-a-bed bedding, rise-and-fall bedding, both, like sleeping in a hotel, Walker's Crisps, Le Creuset butter dish, Arga pan set, M&S food shop, hourglass bronzer, and Kerastase Mask Intense conditioner.

items.transcript: found the most incredible skirt and I saw it on the website. I thought, oh that's very Saint Laurent. I'm going to order that because I just love the sort of, I don't know what you'd call it, the sheer vibe. I thought, wow, you could totally wear that with heels or flats and like so many different tops. It's such a perfect colour. I did a massive haul yesterday. Things were selling out as they were in my basket. So I'm going to say for this, because it is so Saint Laurent coded, I am going to say run, don't walk, but just get it and make a decision on it when you get home.

items.transcript: This is like this cool blanket. This side looks like this. This is what I was trying to describe the other day. And then this is the, it's just cold. This is the cold side. So lovely. Both sides very nice and soft. Both sides keep cool. Don't know how it works. Can't explain it, but it's fab. This is a massive one. They do come in different sizes and there's a couple of colors.

Ad | Straight hair is back, and the Olaplex No.7 Bonding Oil is one of my favourite products for achieving a sleek, shiny finish. ✨

I use it 3 ways:

✔️ Before heat styling as a heat protectant
✔️ After styling for extra shine and smoothness
✔️ Throughout the week to tame frizz and refresh my hair

If you’re looking for a lightweight hair oil that adds shine without leaving your hair greasy, this is worth trying.

Have you tried Olaplex No.7 Bonding Oil yet?

items.transcript: Straight hair is back, but the secret to making it look expensive is I use Olaplex No. 7 bonding oil three different... Firstly, did you know it was a heat protector? It protects up to 450 degrees Fahrenheit. Once my hair is straightened, I use two to three drops to control any frizz and flyaways, keeping that sleek, straight look. Lastly, I keep it with me just in case I need to freshen up my hair in the week.
